name: CRUD/conditional-delete
description: Conditional delete (DELETE /Patient?search-params) conformance tests: rejection with no criteria, idempotent no-op on no match, single-match delete, and 412 on multiple matches (with an optional bulk-delete extension).

variable.name: oneMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient unconditionally created in setup that carries identifier CD-ONEMATCH

operation.description: Unconditionally create the Patient that the single-match delete test will remove (CD-ONEMATCH)
Unconditionally create the first of two Patients sharing identifier CD-MULTI
Unconditionally create the second of two Patients sharing identifier CD-MULTI

test.name: Conditional delete with no search criteria is rejected
test.description: DELETE /Patient with no search criteria must be rejected with either 400 Bad Request or 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient with no query string

assert.description: Primary: expect 412 Precondition Failed (warningOnly: some servers return 400 instead)
Alternative: 400 Bad Request is also accepted

test.name: Conditional delete with no matching resources succeeds
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=... matching no existing resource; the delete is a no-op and the server must still return a 2xx success status (idempotent delete).

assert.description: Must return a 2xx success status (204 No Content is typical)

test.name: Conditional delete with exactly one matching resource removes it
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=... matching exactly one existing Patient; the server must delete it (2xx), and a subsequent read must return either 404 Not Found or 410 Gone.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-ONEMATCH, matches the single seeded Patient
Attempt to read the deleted Patient

assert.description: Alternative: 404 Not Found is also accepted

test.name: Conditional delete with multiple matching resources fails
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=... matching two existing Patients with no multi-delete opt-in; the server must return 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-MULTI, matches both seeded Patients, no multi-delete opt-in

test.name: Conditional delete can remove multiple matches when the server opts in via _count
test.description: DELETE /Patient?identifier=...&_count=100 opting into bulk delete of two matching Patients; expected to succeed (2xx) on servers supporting this non-standard extension (warningOnly, since it isn't mandated by the base FHIR spec).

operation.description: Conditional DELETE /Patient?identifier=...CD-MULTI&_count=100, opting into bulk delete of both matches

assert.description: Server-extension: expect a 2xx success status when bulk conditional delete is supported

operation.description: Teardown: conditional delete the CD-NOMATCH Patient (should already be absent)
Teardown: conditional delete the CD-ONEMATCH Patient (should already be deleted by the tests)
Teardown: conditional delete any remaining CD-MULTI Patients (best effort, in case the server does not support bulk conditional delete)
